spec_match.rationale: Compares inline <script type=speculationrules> delivery with the HTTP Speculation-Rules response header under selectable CSP presets.

interactive.rationale: The CSP picker refreshes the emitted server headers and the run button opens a top-level probe that records Chrome's rule-document request.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The card promises a pick-a-CSP verdict view and the page now distinguishes simulated inline-script blocking from observed header behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The live route was verified under the locked script-src 'none' preset and continued to fetch the rule document.

design_system.rationale: Uses shared variables, associated form labels, and route status styling with clear pass/fail states.

summary: Resolved the real-fetch follow-up. Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/148.0.0.0 verified /v131/exempt-speculation-rules-header-from-csp-restrictions/csp-tester/?verify=after-real-route: changing to script-src 'none' emitted the restrictive CSP and Speculation-Rules header, the top-level probe recorded /rules.json, the header verdict updated to allowed, and no console errors were present.
